epic.name: Slack Integration
epic.objective: Implement Slack channel creation and message posting for incidents using the ChatService interface pattern
epic.definition_of_done: When an incident is created, a Slack channel is auto-created with naming convention inc-{number}-{slug}, and incident details are posted as the first message

tasks.name: Define ChatService interface
tasks.description: Create abstract interface for chat operations as per ADR-005 to enable future Teams support
acceptance_criteria: ChatService interface defined with: CreateChannel, PostMessage, UpdateMessage, ArchiveChannel methods
Channel struct with ID, Name, URL fields
Message struct with Text, Blocks, ThreadTS fields
Interface documented with expected behavior

tasks.name: Implement SlackService with slack-go SDK
tasks.description: Create Slack implementation of ChatService using slack-go library
acceptance_criteria: SlackService struct implements ChatService interface
Configured with SLACK_BOT_TOKEN from environment
Connection validated on startup
Proper error handling for Slack API errors
Rate limiting handled with exponential backoff

tasks.name: Implement Slack channel creation
tasks.description: Create channels following naming convention inc-{incident_number}-{slug}
acceptance_criteria: Channel name formatted as inc-{number}-{slug} (e.g., inc-042-api-gateway-errors)
Channel name truncated to Slack's 80 char limit
Channel name sanitized (lowercase, hyphens only)
Returns channel ID and name
Handles channel name collision by appending suffix
Channel created as public by default

tasks.name: Implement incident message formatting
tasks.description: Create rich Slack message with incident details and action buttons
acceptance_criteria: Message includes: emoji indicator, incident number, title, severity, status, created timestamp
Severity displayed with appropriate emoji (red circle critical, orange high, yellow medium, green low)
Linked alerts listed with source and title
Action buttons: Acknowledge, Resolve (using Slack Block Kit)
Message formatted using Slack Block Kit for rich display

tasks.name: Connect incident creation to Slack channel creation
tasks.description: Integrate Slack channel creation into incident creation flow
acceptance_criteria: When incident is created, Slack channel is created asynchronously
Incident record updated with slack_channel_id and slack_channel_name
Initial message posted to channel after creation
Timeline entry added: type=slack_channel_created
Failure to create Slack channel does not fail incident creation
Slack errors logged with incident context

tasks.name: Implement async Slack operations via Redis queue
tasks.description: Queue Slack API calls through Redis to handle rate limits and failures as per ADR-007
acceptance_criteria: SlackJob struct with type, incident_id, payload
Jobs queued to Redis list
Worker process consumes jobs and executes Slack calls
Failed jobs retried with exponential backoff (max 3 attempts)
Dead letter handling for permanently failed jobs
Job status trackable

tasks.name: Add Slack configuration validation
tasks.description: Validate Slack credentials on startup and provide clear error messages
acceptance_criteria: On startup, verify SLACK_BOT_TOKEN is valid by calling auth.test
Verify bot has required scopes: channels:manage, channels:read, chat:write
Clear error message if token invalid or missing scopes
Application starts without Slack if SLACK_BOT_TOKEN not set (degraded mode)
Log warning when running in degraded mode

epic.completion_notes: All tasks completed. Additional enhancement: Auto-invite users feature added (SLACK_AUTO_INVITE_USER_IDS env var) to automatically invite configured users to incident channels. Platform-agnostic implementation ready for Teams support in v0.8.
